articles.title: Health advocates warn government’s claims of baby formula safety contradict data
articles.description: Independent scientists who reviewed the results said most samples were contaminated with Pfas or phthalates The Trump administration announced earlier this month that hundreds of baby formula samples it tested for toxic chemicals “meet a high safety standard”, but public health advocates warn this claim contradicts data showing a majority were contaminated with dangerous substances, such as Pfas or phthalates. Independent scientists who reviewed the results say the data gaps and the contamination raise concerns, though they added the testing shows some bright spots, and praised the US Food and Drug Administration for expanding the testing program, then making the results public. Continue reading...

articles.title: Antarctica is melting from below and scientists say it’s worse than expected
articles.description: Scientists have uncovered a hidden Antarctic threat that could accelerate global sea level rise far faster than expected. Deep beneath floating ice shelves, long channels carved into the ice appear to trap warmer ocean water, dramatically speeding up melting from below. Even regions of East Antarctica once considered relatively stable may be far more vulnerable than scientists realized. Researchers warn that current climate models may be missing this dangerous process entirely, meaning future sea level rise could be underestimated.

articles.title: Hantavirus is not easily spread, but is global heating upping our exposure?
articles.description: Argentina back in spotlight 30 years after first person-to-person transmission was documented in Patagonia An outbreak in rural communities 30 years ago in the Patagonia area of Argentina led scientists, for the first time, to document person-to-person transmission of hantavirus, which until then had been known only to spread through contact with rodents. Nearly a decade ago another outbreak, also in Patagonia, provided detailed evidence of inter-human transmission when an infected 68-year-old rural worker attended a birthday party in a small village. The infection spread and resulted in 11 deaths. Continue reading...

articles.title: Cebu City eyes 'green' makeover for Fuente Osmeña Circle
articles.description: LAPU-LAPU CITY, Cebu — Cebu City is eyeing a greener, more pedestrian-friendly Fuente Osmeña Circle as officials advance a rehabilitation plan focused on safety, accessibility, and environmental design. The project follows several long-standing concerns about deteriorating infrastructure and inefficient public use of the space. In an update from the Cebu City Public Information Office, Mayor Nestor Archival met with representatives of the Cebu Chamber of Commerce and Industry on May 5 to align priorities and review proposed interventions. Stakeholders used the meeting to evaluate existing site conditions and identify practical improvements aimed at making the space safer and more functional [...]... Keep on reading: Cebu City eyes 'green' makeover for Fuente Osmeña Circle

articles.title: Storm expected to weaken, trigger rains in Visayas, Mindanao on Sunday
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines – Tropical Storm Caloy (international name: Hagupit) is forecast to weaken to a tropical depression but its trough or extension may trigger rains in the Visayas and Mindanao on Sunday evening, the state weather bureau said. “Caloy will not have a direct impact on any part of the country, but later its trough or extension could possibly bring rains to parts of Visayas and Mindanao,” Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) weather specialist Veronica Torres said in Filipino in a 5 a.m. update. READ: Tropical Storm ‘Hagupit’ enters PAR, given local name ‘Caloy’ Caloy was last [...]... Keep on reading: Storm expected to weaken, trigger rains in Visayas, Mindanao on Sunday
